title: Revisiting Preferences and Argumentation
abstract: The ASPIC+ framework is intermediate in abstraction between Dung's argumentation framework and concrete instantiating logics. This paper generalises ASPIC+ to accommodate classical logic instantiations, and adopts a new proposal for evaluating extensions: attacks are not defeats until deciding conflict-free sets, while defeats are exclusively used to determine the acceptability of arguments. Key properties and rationality postulates are then shown to hold for the new framework.
